channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Crowds flock to newest Catholic saint in Assisi
item.description: ASSISI, Italy (AP) — Pilgrims have been pouring into this medieval hilltop town to venerate not only two of the Catholic Church’s most celebrated saints, Francis and Clare, but its newest — Carlo Acutis, the first millennial saint, who will be canonized on April 27. “St. Francis, St.
item.dc:creator: GIOVANNA DELL'ORTO, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Brazilian Supreme Court panel orders ex-President Bolsonaro to stand trial over alleged coup plan
item.description: RIO DE JANEIRO (AP) — A panel of Brazil Supreme Court justices on Wednesday unanimously accepted charges against former President Jair Bolsonaro over an alleged attempt to stay in office after his 2022 election defeat, and ordered him to stand trial.
item.dc:creator: ELÉONORE HUGHES, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: What is known about the deadly air crash between a passenger jet and a US Army helicopter
item.description: Congress is holding a hearing Thursday about the January collision between an American Airlines passenger jet and an Army helicopter that killed all 67 people aboard both aircraft.
item.dc:creator: Josh Funk, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: States move to ban or restrict use of synthetic dyes used in candies and cereal
item.description: Synthetic dyes used to make brightly colored cereals, drinks and candies are coming under scrutiny in states across the country.
item.dc:creator: Leah Willingham, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Pilot rescued with 2 girls from icy Alaska lake was not authorized to have passengers, official says
item.description: ANCHORAGE, Alaska (AP) — A pilot rescued with two young family members after they survived a night on the wing of an airplane partially submerged in an Alaska lake is a student pilot who was not authorized to fly with passengers, according to a U.S. official and federal aviation records.
item.dc:creator: MARK THIESSEN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Washington researchers warn of serious decline in honey bee colonies in 2025
item.description: Losses of commercial honey bee colonies are projected to be the highest on record in the last decade, according to Washington State University researchers.
item.dc:creator: Jenna Deml, KREX 5

item.title: ICE arrestees claim they were targeted for protest involvement
item.description: Students who acknowledge they participated in pro-Palestinian protests are being targeted by ICE, a complaint says
item.dc:creator: Jeff Arnold, NewsNation

item.title: The White House security breach raises concerns among some military spouses and veterans
item.description: NORFOLK, Va. (AP) — When Alyssa Myatt’s husband served on an aircraft carrier last year, she and other U.S. Navy spouses had to follow strict security protocols that meant driving to the ship’s home port just to learn that its deployment was being extended.
item.dc:creator: BEN FINLEY and ALLEN G. BREED, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Chewing gum releases thousands of microplastics into body, study finds
item.description: A recent study found that chewing gum can release up to thousands of microplastics per piece and potentially be ingested, according to researchers.
item.dc:creator: Rhea Caoile, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Chewing gum releases thousands of microplastics into body, study finds
item.description: A recent study found that chewing gum can release up to thousands of microplastics per piece and potentially be ingested, according to researchers.
item.dc:creator: Rhea Caoile, KRON4

item.title: Trump places 25% tariff on imported autos, expecting to raise $100 billion in tax revenues
item.description: White House press secretary Karoline Leavitt said the tariffs would be detailed at a 4 p.m. EST news conference.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Parents push for change after sexual assault case at child care center
item.description: "Three years later, you're still having to go to court, or get therapy. So it's not just it happens and then it's over with," Amber Chatelain said.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Al-Shaikh, NewsNation

item.title: Ex-NFL player charged after 190 dogs seized from dog fighting venture
item.description: An Oklahoma man is facing federal charges after officials seized 190 dogs from an animal fighting venture, according to court documents.
item.dc:creator: Kyler Swaim, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Loved ones search for missing 21-year-old Southern California man
item.description: Emmanuel Boamah, 21, is described as a man standing 5 feet 10 inches tall and weighing around 150 pounds.
item.dc:creator: Vivian Chow, NewsNation

item.title: Gene Hackman, Betsy Arakawa death: Footage from outside home released
item.description: The footage suggests that one of the top priorities for responding deputies was the couple's privacy.
item.dc:creator: Annalisa Pardo, NewsNation

item.title: A year after the Baltimore bridge collapse, a long road to recovery is ahead
item.description: BALTIMORE (AP) — A year after the catastrophic collapse of Baltimore’s Francis Scott Key Bridge, Maryland leaders are honoring the six construction workers who were killed when the road they were repairing buckled underneath them.
item.dc:creator: LEA SKENE and BRIAN WITTE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Dollar Tree sells Family Dollar for $1 billion
item.description: Dollar Tree has agreed to sell Family Dollar for $1 billion, ending the short-lived merger deal and allowing both companies to focus on their core operations.
item.dc:creator: Joshua Hallenbeck, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Atlantic publishes Trump Cabinet group chat messages
item.description: The Atlantic has published the Signal group chat messages among national security leaders that were inadvertently shared with Editor-in-Chief Jeffrey Goldberg, noting administration officials said Tuesday they were not classified.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, KRON4

item.title: How the Signal 'group chat' furor has affected the 5 biggest players
item.description: The fact that such inherently sensitive information was being communicated in such a forum — and in front of a reporter whom no other participant appears to have noticed — has caused a political storm.
item.dc:creator: Niall Stanage, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Vance to accompany wife to Greenland amid backlash
item.description: Vance’s announcement adds another layer of tension to an already fraught atmosphere surrounding his wife’s planned visit.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Bureau of Indian Affairs Missing and Murdered Unit investigating alleged military base killing
item.description: The Bureau of Indian Affairs Missing and Murdered Unit aids in the arrest, and indictment of an active-duty airman accused of murdering a 21-year-old tribal woman at an Air Force base.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: iPad submerged 5 years in London's River Thames helps unlock murder plot
item.description: The iPad was covered in dark mud after five years at the bottom of England's longest river, the Thames, but it was the chip inside that helped police crack a five-year-old murder plot.
item.dc:creator: KTLA 5

item.title: Mia Love's family announces funeral plans
item.description: The family of Mia Love has announced funeral plans for the former Utah congresswoman, who passed away Sunday, March 23, at the age of 49, after battling brain cancer.
item.dc:creator: Trevor Myers, ABC 4

item.title: Pilot and 2 young daughters survive the night on airplane wing after crashing into icy Alaska lake
item.description: ANCHORAGE, Alaska (AP) — A pilot and his two young daughters survived on the wing of a plane for about 12 hours after it crashed and was partially submerged in an icy Alaska lake, then were rescued after being spotted by a good Samaritan.
item.dc:creator: MARK THIESSEN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Iowa law banning school library books that depict sex acts on hold again after a new federal ruling
item.description: DES MOINES, Iowa (AP) — Iowa cannot, for now, continue to enforce part of its book ban law, a federal judge said Tuesday, giving major publishers that sued the state the second temporary reprieve they requested. The new decision from U.S.
item.dc:creator: HANNAH FINGERHUT, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Los Angeles Dodgers to make Trump White House visit next month
item.description: (KTLA) -- The Los Angeles Dodgers plan to visit President Donald Trump at the White House to celebrate their World Series victory, the team announced Tuesday. The team released a statement Tuesday afternoon that said it would visit the White House "in keeping with long-standing baseball tradition.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, KRON4

item.title: Video and 911 calls played by police detail chaos from deadly shooting in New Mexico
item.description: Police released video footage and 911 emergency calls Tuesday depicting chilling scenes of people running in panic as gunfire crackled in a New Mexico park where three people were killed and 15 others were wounded during an unauthorized car show.
item.dc:creator: RIO YAMAT and SUSAN MONTOYA BRYAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Family of Riley Strain files wrongful death lawsuit against Delta Chi fraternity
item.description: Family members of Riley Strain, a University of Missouri student who died last year during a group trip to Nashville, Tennessee, have filed a wrongful death lawsuit against his former fraternity and pledge brothers.
item.dc:creator: Joey Schneider, WFLA, NBC

item.title: President Trump pardons former Hunter Biden business partner Devon Archer
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump on Tuesday pardoned a former business partner of Hunter Biden who was convicted of participating in a conspiracy to defraud a Native American tribe.
item.dc:creator: ALANNA DURKIN RICHER and DARLENE SUPERVILLE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: More states move to ban cellphones in school as Georgia is latest to act
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — More states are moving to ban or restrict cellphones in school, a trend that continues to win bipartisan support in legislatures.
item.dc:creator: JEFF AMY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump signs order targeting law firm that employed Mueller team prosecutor
item.description: President Trump signed an order Tuesday targeting a law firm that previously employed Andrew Weissmann, a former federal prosecutor who worked on then-special counsel Robert Mueller’s team.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, Fox 5

item.title: FBI launches Tesla vandalism task force
item.description: The task force seeks to counter the violent activity targeting Tesla vehicles and charging stations in recent weeks.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, WGN 9

item.title: Tennessee pitcher Karlyn Pickens throws fastest pitch in college softball history
item.description: Tennessee softball standout Karlyn Pickens wrote her name into the history books on Monday with a record-breaking pitch.
item.dc:creator: Gregory Raucoules, KRON4

item.title: Waymo plans to bring its driverless taxis to Washington in 2026
item.description: Waymo on Tuesday added Washington to its pioneering robotaxi service's steadily expanding list of U.S. markets.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Student returns lost backpack filled with luxury jewelry, diamonds, cash
item.description: Amid the fog of international travel, a group of Jewish students studying in Jerusalem was handed a backpack -- that none recognized as their own.
item.dc:creator: Anthony DiLorenzo, KRON4

item.title: Live updates: Ratcliffe, Gabbard pressed on war plans Signal group chat
item.description: The Trump administration is scrambling to contain the fallout from a bombshell Atlantic article revealing its editor-in-chief had been mistakenly included in a Signal group chat involving national security adviser Mike Waltz, Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth, intelligence leadership and other Trump ad
item.dc:creator: The Hill staff, Fox 5

item.title: Columbia student sues Trump admin over efforts to deport her
item.description: Columbia University student Yunseo Chung is suing the Trump administration amid its efforts to deport the 21-year-old, who has been in the U.S. since she was 7 and has lawful permanent resident status.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, WFLA, NBC
